volumes: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: What Is Compression?
chapters.brief: The grand tour and the promise. What compression is, the model+coder idea in plain words, why it silently underpins the entire digital world (streaming, the web, phones, genomes, AI). The book's contract with the reader: from zero, assuming only 9th-grade math, with skippable Go-Further boxes. Introduce tinyzip and the running scoreboard.
chapters.tinyzip: introduce the project; no code yet

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: A Guided History of the Field
chapters.brief: A narrative arc from telegraphy to June 2026, naming the milestones and people we will deepen later. Orientation, not detail — a map of the journey so every later chapter has a place to hang.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Information, Data, and Symbols
chapters.brief: What 'data' really is: signs, symbols, alphabets, messages; analog vs digital; the idea of redundancy and why it is the thing we remove. Sets intuitions before any math.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Numbers and Number Systems
chapters.brief: From counting to place value to binary, octal, hexadecimal, and two's complement — taught from scratch with worked conversions. Why computers use base 2.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Logic and Boolean Algebra
chapters.brief: Propositions, truth, AND/OR/NOT/XOR, truth tables, De Morgan's laws, logic gates, and how bits implement logic. The reasoning backbone of computer science, built from zero.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Sets, Functions, and Relations
chapters.brief: The grammar of mathematics: sets and membership, subsets, unions/intersections, functions and mappings, injections/bijections (needed for codes), relations. Worked, concrete, friendly.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Exponents, Logarithms, and Growth
chapters.brief: The single most important tool in the book. Exponents, the three exponent rules, logarithms as inverse exponents, the multiplication-into-addition magic, change of base, and why a bit is a logarithm.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Counting and Combinatorics
chapters.brief: Permutations, combinations, the multiplication principle, and the counting/pigeonhole bound — the proof that no compressor shrinks everything. Foundations of why limits exist.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Probability From Scratch
chapters.brief: Built from coins and dice: sample spaces, events, probability axioms, conditional probability, independence, and Bayes' rule. Every idea with a numeric example.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Random Variables, Expectation, and Variance
chapters.brief: Random variables as numbered outcomes; expectation as a weighted average (the cost of a code!); variance and spread; the geometric and Bernoulli sources we will compress.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Sequences, Sums, and a Gentle Calculus
chapters.brief: ∑ and ∏ notation demystified; sequences and limits; the bare minimum of derivatives, slopes, optimization and gradients (for later RDO and neural nets) — taught visually and from scratch.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Vectors, Matrices, and Linear Transformations
chapters.brief: Vectors as arrows and lists, dot products, matrices as transformations, basis and orthogonality — the machinery behind transforms (DCT/wavelets) and neural networks, built gently.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: How Computers Represent Things
chapters.brief: Bits, bytes, words; unsigned/signed integers; characters, ASCII and Unicode/UTF-8; floating point; endianness. How real data becomes the bytes we compress.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Algorithms, Data Structures, and Complexity
chapters.brief: What an algorithm is; pseudocode; Big-O notation from scratch; arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, hash tables, trees, heaps, priority queues — the toolbox every codec uses.
chapters.tinyzip: data structures the project will rely on

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: A Python Primer I: Values, Types, and Control Flow
chapters.brief: Python 3.14 from zero: the REPL, values and variables, int/float/str/bool, operators, f-strings, if/elif/else, while/for, ranges, and the walrus operator. Every concept in a gopython box with runnable code.
chapters.tinyzip: set up the project skeleton and first scripts

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: A Python Primer II: Data Structures, Functions, and Iterators
chapters.brief: Lists, tuples, dicts, sets; slicing; comprehensions; functions, arguments, type hints (dict[int,int]); modules; iterators and generators; match statements. Read-Python fluency, Python 3.14.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: A Python Primer III: Bytes, Bits, Files, and Binary I/O
chapters.brief: bytes vs str, bytearray, indexing bytes, bit operations (& | ^ << >>), reading/writing binary files, struct, a from-scratch BitWriter/BitReader. The toolkit tinyzip needs to emit real compressed files.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Shannon and the Birth of Information Theory
chapters.brief: 1948 in depth: self-information/surprisal, entropy H(X), joint and conditional entropy, the chain rule — all from the probability we just built, with intuition (entropy as 'twenty questions').

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: The Source Coding Theorem
chapters.brief: Why entropy is the floor: prefix codes, the Kraft–McMillan inequality, typical sets and the AEP, and the noiseless source coding theorem — stated, proved gently, and made intuitive.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Mutual Information, Channels, and the Noisy-Channel Theorem
chapters.brief: KL divergence, mutual information, channel capacity, the noisy-channel coding theorem, and source–channel separation — the duality of removing vs adding redundancy.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Rate–Distortion: The Theory of Lossy Compression
chapters.brief: Shannon's R(D): distortion measures, the rate–distortion function, the Gaussian case, reverse water-filling, Blahut–Arimoto, and a first look at the rate–distortion–perception tradeoff.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Kolmogorov Complexity and Algorithmic Information
chapters.brief: Solomonoff/Kolmogorov/Chaitin: descriptive complexity, the invariance theorem, incompressibility, uncomputability, algorithmic probability and the universal prior — the ideal compressor we can never build.

chapters.volume: Volume I - Foundations: The Language of Information
chapters.title: Compression = Prediction = Learning
chapters.brief: The thesis that powers the rest of the book: cross-entropy = expected code length, MDL (Rissanen 1978), universal coding, and why a great predictor is a great compressor. The hinge into the modern era.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Entropy Coding I: Shannon–Fano and Huffman
chapters.brief: Optimal symbol codes: Shannon–Fano and its suboptimality; Huffman's 1952 greedy construction; proof of optimality among prefix codes; canonical and length-limited Huffman; adaptive Huffman.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Integer and Universal Codes
chapters.brief: Unary, Elias gamma/delta/omega, Golomb–Rice (optimal for geometric sources), Exp-Golomb, Fibonacci coding, byte varints — coding when you know the shape but not exact probabilities.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Arithmetic Coding
chapters.brief: Reaching the entropy limit by coding a whole message as one interval: Elias's idea, Pasco/Rissanen, Witten–Neal–Cleary 1987, finite-precision renormalization and carry handling, binary AC, CABAC/MQ. The patent saga.
chapters.tinyzip: add an arithmetic coder to tinyzip

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Asymmetric Numeral Systems (ANS)
chapters.brief: Duda's 2009–2013 breakthrough: the numeral-system intuition, uABS, rANS and tANS, the LIFO nature, interleaving/SIMD, normalization — arithmetic-coding ratio at Huffman speed. Adoption and patent fights.
chapters.tinyzip: swap the entropy stage to rANS

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Dictionary Coding I: LZ77 and LZ78
chapters.brief: Ziv–Lempel: LZ77 sliding-window matches, LZ78 explicit dictionaries, LZSS, and the universality/asymptotic-optimality results. The idea behind almost every general-purpose codec.
chapters.tinyzip: build an LZ77 match finder + parser

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Dictionary Coding II: LZW and the Patent Wars
chapters.brief: Welch's 1984 LZW; compress, GIF, TIFF, V.42bis; the Unisys patent (1985) and the GIF licensing firestorm that created PNG; the wider LZ family map.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: DEFLATE, zlib, gzip, and PNG
chapters.brief: The most-deployed compressor ever: DEFLATE = LZ77 + Huffman, block structure, fixed/dynamic codes, the 32 KB window, hash-chain match finding; zlib/gzip/ZIP/PNG and HTTP ubiquity.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: LZMA and the High-Ratio Dictionary Coders
chapters.brief: 7-Zip's LZMA: large dictionaries, range coding, context-modelled literals, optimal parsing; LZMA2, PPMd, .xz — and the 2024 xz-utils backdoor as a supply-chain cautionary tale.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: The Modern Frontier: Zstandard, Brotli, LZ4, Snappy
chapters.brief: The speed/ratio Pareto revolution: LZ4 and Snappy (speed), Brotli (2015, static dictionary), Zstandard (2016: levels, trained dictionaries, long-distance matching, ANS) — why zstd won everywhere.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Statistical Modeling: PPM and Context Mixing
chapters.brief: Beyond dictionaries: PPM (1984), escape mechanisms, exclusion; the leap to context mixing — logistic mixing of many predictors, SSE/APM, neural mixing.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: The Ratio Champions: PAQ, ZPAQ, and cmix
chapters.brief: Mahoney's PAQ lineage, the anatomy of a context-mixing compressor, ZPAQ's archival format, cmix's ratio crown and memory cost — and the ratio-vs-speed wall.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: The Burrows–Wheeler Transform and bzip2
chapters.brief: The 1994 reversible clustering permutation, the inverse, Move-To-Front, RLE, the bzip2 pipeline; suffix arrays and the FM-index, and BWT's second life in genomic alignment.

chapters.volume: Volume II - Classical Lossless Compression
chapters.title: Benchmarks, Corpora, and the Hutter Prize
chapters.brief: How the field measures itself: Calgary/Canterbury/Silesia corpora, the Large Text Compression Benchmark, enwik8/9, Squash/lzbench; the Hutter Prize and 'compression as AI', with the record progression.
chapters.tinyzip: benchmark tinyzip on a real corpus

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Signals, Sampling, and the Frequency Domain
chapters.brief: DSP from scratch: continuous vs discrete signals, the sampling theorem, aliasing, sine waves and frequency, the DFT/FFT and the time–frequency view — the basis of every transform codec.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Transforms for Compression: KLT, DCT, Wavelets, MDCT
chapters.brief: The heart of lossy coding: the KLT as the ideal decorrelator, the DCT (1974) as its practical proxy, wavelets and multiresolution, the MDCT and overlap/perfect reconstruction. Energy compaction, intuitively and mathematically.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Quantization: From Scalar to Vector
chapters.brief: The lossy step: uniform/non-uniform scalar quantization, Lloyd–Max, companding, dithering, dead-zones; vector quantization, the LBG algorithm, structured/trellis VQ — and the VQ→VQ-VAE→product-quantization lineage.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Prediction and Differential Coding
chapters.brief: DPCM, delta and adaptive delta modulation, linear prediction (LPC), the normal equations and Levinson–Durbin, predictors in JPEG-LS — exploiting correlation before coding.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Rate–Distortion Optimization in Practice
chapters.brief: How real encoders spend bits: Lagrangian RDO (J = D + λR), operational R–D curves, bit allocation, rate control (CBR/VBR/CRF), two-pass and perceptual extensions.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: JPEG: Anatomy of the World's Image Codec
chapters.brief: Full pipeline: YCbCr, chroma subsampling, the 8×8 DCT, quantization tables, zig-zag, run-length + Huffman; why blocking/ringing appear; progressive vs baseline; JPEG-LS lossless. A 1992 standard that won't die.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: JPEG 2000 and the Wavelet Promise
chapters.brief: Wavelets in practice: EBCOT, code-blocks, bit-plane coding, scalability, the MQ coder; genuinely better than JPEG yet beaten on the web — and why it rules cinema and medicine.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Lossless and Simple Image Formats: GIF, PNG, QOI
chapters.brief: GIF (LZW, palettes, animation), PNG (DEFLATE + filters, the patent-driven origin), and the minimalist QOI (2021). Where lossless/palette formats still rule.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: The Modern Image Wars: WebP, HEIC, AVIF, JPEG XL
chapters.brief: Intra-frame video codecs as still formats: WebP (VP8), HEIC (HEVC), AVIF (AV1); then JPEG XL — modular+VarDCT, lossless JPEG transcoding, ANS, royalty-free, and its Chrome removal then Feb-2026 re-addition.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Psychoacoustics: Compressing for the Ear
chapters.brief: The science codecs exploit: the basilar membrane, critical bands, the threshold of hearing, simultaneous and temporal masking, perceptual entropy — deciding which sounds are inaudible.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: MP3 and the MPEG Audio Lineage
chapters.brief: MPEG-1 Layers I/II/III, the hybrid filterbank (polyphase + MDCT), the psychoacoustic model, the bit reservoir, Huffman tables; Fraunhofer, the patents, and MP3's cultural earthquake.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: AAC, Vorbis, and Modern General Audio
chapters.brief: AAC's improvements (MDCT-only, TNS, PNS, SBR/HE-AAC, parametric stereo); royalty-free Vorbis; USAC/xHE-AAC; the licensing landscape.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Opus: One Codec to Rule Real-Time Audio
chapters.brief: The IETF triumph (2012): SILK+CELT hybrid, sub-26 ms latency, 6–510 kbit/s, the codec behind WebRTC/Zoom/Discord; Opus 1.3–1.5 and its ML features (DRED, neural PLC).

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: Lossless and Speech Coding
chapters.brief: Lossless audio: FLAC (LPC + Rice), ALAC, WavPack, Monkey's Audio, and why lossless reaches only ~50–60%. Speech: LPC-10, CELP, ACELP, the vocoder idea, AMR/EVS.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: The Hybrid Video Codec: Motion, Prediction, Transform
chapters.brief: Why video is mostly redundancy: temporal prediction and block motion compensation, I/P/B frames, the GOP, intra prediction, the transform+quantize+entropy loop, in-loop filtering, encoder/decoder asymmetry.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: H.261 to H.264/AVC: The Workhorse Era
chapters.brief: The lineage: H.261, MPEG-1, MPEG-2/H.262 (DVD/broadcast), H.263, MPEG-4 Part 2, then H.264/AVC (2003): variable blocks, multi-reference, quarter-pel, CAVLC/CABAC, profiles. Why AVC still dominates.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: HEVC and VVC: More Compression, More Patents
chapters.brief: H.265/HEVC (2013): CTUs, quadtrees, ~50% over AVC and its patent quagmire; H.266/VVC (2020): bigger gains, screen content, 360/HDR. The licensing lessons.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: The Open Codecs: VP8, VP9, AV1, AV2
chapters.brief: On2/Google, the Alliance for Open Media, AV1 (2018) tools (CDEF, restoration, warped motion), encoder complexity and hardware rollout, AV2 (2025–26), and the open-vs-licensed war.

chapters.volume: Volume III - Lossy and Perceptual Media Compression
chapters.title: The Successor Frontier and Encoding at Scale
chapters.brief: JVET's ECM (>25% over VVC), NN video coding (NNVC), H.267 (~2028); and the practical layer: Netflix per-title/per-shot Dynamic Optimizer, content-adaptive encoding, VMAF-driven RDO.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: A Machine-Learning Primer for Compression
chapters.brief: Just enough deep learning, from scratch: neurons as functions, gradient descent (building on the gentle calculus), autoencoders and the bottleneck, VAEs/GANs/diffusion/autoregressive models — all as ways to learn the source distribution.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Learned Image Compression: Autoencoders and Hyperpriors
chapters.brief: The Ballé line (2016–18): end-to-end RD-optimized transforms, GDN, quantization-as-noise, the scale/mean hyperprior, autoregressive/context entropy models (Minnen 2018, Cheng 2020) — overtaking JPEG/AVIF.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Generative and Perceptual Codecs
chapters.brief: Optimizing realism not MSE: the rate–distortion–perception tradeoff, GAN codecs (HiFiC), diffusion decoders (PerCo 2024, ultra-low bitrate), and the 2025–26 one-step/real-time push. The 'hallucinated detail' debate.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Learned Video and Implicit Neural Representations
chapters.brief: Neural video coding (DVC 2019, the DCVC line, VCT); implicit neural representations (COIN, NeRF-as-compression, video INRs); promise vs decode-complexity/standardization reality.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Neural Audio Codecs as Tokenizers
chapters.brief: SoundStream (2021), EnCodec (2022), DAC (2023): residual vector quantization, the discrete-token substrate now powering audio LLMs and TTS — codec and tokenizer converge.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Compression as Intelligence: The Theory
chapters.brief: Why generalization equals compression: MDL revisited, PAC-Bayes, the Hutter thesis, the Solomonoff ideal, the AIXI sketch — and the honest counterarguments.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Large Language Models as Compressors
chapters.brief: The mechanics: an autoregressive LM gives P(next token); plus arithmetic coding it becomes a SOTA lossless compressor. DeepMind's 'Language Modeling Is Compression' (2023), scaling-as-compression, the tokenizer caveat; Bellard's nncp and ts_zip.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Model Compression I: Quantization
chapters.brief: Compressing the network: post-training quantization (GPTQ, AWQ), QAT, FP8/INT4, outliers; and the information-theoretic frontier — ternary BitNet b1.58 (log2 3 ≈ 1.58 bits/weight), native low-bit training, BitNet v2.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: Model Compression II: Pruning, Distillation, Low-Rank
chapters.brief: Magnitude/structured/movement pruning, the Lottery Ticket Hypothesis, knowledge distillation, low-rank factorization and LoRA — squeezing a frontier model onto a phone.

chapters.volume: Volume IV - The Neural and AI Era
chapters.title: KV-Cache, Context, and Embedding Compression
chapters.brief: Inference-time compression: KV-cache eviction (H2O), DeepSeek MLA, and the 2025–26 vector-quantization frontier (TurboQuant, KVTC); prompt/context compression; product quantization, OPQ, binary/Matryoshka embeddings for retrieval.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Scientific and Floating-Point Compression
chapters.brief: HPC's data deluge: lossless FP (FPC, Blosc, byte-split, bit-packing); error-bounded lossy zfp and SZ with absolute/relative bounds; the lossless-vs-error-bounded distinction.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Columnar and Database Compression
chapters.brief: The analytics toolkit: dictionary encoding, run-length, frame-of-reference and delta-FOR, bit-packing, Roaring bitmaps; how Parquet/ORC layer these under zstd; operate-on-compressed query engines.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Time-Series and IoT Compression
chapters.brief: Streaming numeric data: Facebook Gorilla (XOR'd floats, delta-of-delta timestamps), the basis of Prometheus/InfluxDB; simple-8b and stream VByte for integers; sensor constraints.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Genomic and Biological Sequence Compression
chapters.brief: FASTQ/FASTA/SAM/BAM, reference-based compression and CRAM, quality-score modeling (the dominant cost), the BWT/FM-index alignment connection revisited; a compression-bound science.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: DNA Data Storage and Constrained Coding
chapters.brief: Storing bits in molecules: the DNA channel and its constraints (GC balance, homopolymer runs), fountain/LT/Raptor codes, Erlich & Zielinski's DNA Fountain (~215 PB/g), and constrained-coding theory.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Delta, Diff, and Deduplication
chapters.brief: Compression across files and versions: xdelta/bsdiff, VCDIFF, content-defined chunking (Rabin, FastCDC), block/file dedup (Borg, Restic, ZFS), Git packfiles, Docker layers.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: The Error-Correction Boundary
chapters.brief: Adding redundancy back: why compress-then-protect (source–channel separation, and where it fails), CRCs, Reed–Solomon, LDPC, polar codes; the meeting points — DNA storage, QR codes, Opus DRED, flash.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Engineering Fast Codecs
chapters.brief: Where bits-per-second are won: SIMD and interleaved rANS, branchless decoding, cache-aware match finders, multithreading/framing; GPU compression (nvCOMP) and hardware offload (Intel IAA/QAT).

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Compression in the Stack
chapters.brief: Where compression actually runs: HTTP Content-Encoding (gzip→br→zstd), HPACK/QPACK, filesystem compression (Btrfs/ZFS), databases, RAM compression (zram/zswap), the CDN/streaming edge.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Measuring Compression: Metrics and Benchmarks
chapters.brief: Judging codecs honestly: ratio/bpp/bpc, throughput, R–D curves, BD-rate; quality metrics — PSNR's failures, SSIM/MS-SSIM, VMAF, LPIPS/DISTS — and the listening/viewing-test gold standard; benchmark pitfalls.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: A People's History of Compression
chapters.brief: The humans and labs: Shannon, Huffman, Ziv & Lempel, Rissanen, Witten/Neal/Cleary, Burrows & Wheeler, Welch, Katz, Brandenburg, Collet, Duda, Mahoney, Bellard, Ballé, Hutter; Bell, IBM, PARC, Fraunhofer, DEC SRC.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Patents, Standards, and the Politics of Formats
chapters.brief: How law and committees shaped what we use: the arithmetic-coding and LZW patents, MP3 and HEVC pools, MPEG vs AOMedia, the royalty-free movement, the standards bodies — and how patents handed wins to free tech.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Dead Ends, Failures, and Cautionary Tales
chapters.brief: What didn't work and why it teaches: fractal compression's overpromise, infinite-compression scams (and the counting bound that kills them), JPEG 2000's stranded superiority, the xz backdoor. Honest engineering epistemics.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: The State of the Field, June 2026
chapters.brief: A grounded snapshot: what ships in production (zstd, AV1/HEVC, Opus, AVIF/JPEG XL), what's emerging (learned/generative codecs, LLM compression, BitNet, KV-cache VQ), and an honest hype-vs-deployed ledger.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: Open Problems and Grand Challenges
chapters.brief: The frontier: the real limits of learned compression, decode complexity and energy, standardizing neural codecs, semantic/goal-oriented compression, joint source–channel learning, compressing foundation models, coding-for-machines.

chapters.volume: Volume V - Specialized Domains, Systems, and Reflections
chapters.title: The Future of Compression
chapters.brief: Where it heads and why it stays invaluable: data growth vs compute stagnation, compression as the substrate of AI, semantic communication, sustainability, in-storage compute — and a reasoned set of predictions tying the whole book together.
